title: Air-fryer halloumi
subtitle: Sliced, oiled, crisp in seven minutes.
excerpt: Halloumi sliced into thick fingers, brushed with olive oil, cooked at 200°C for seven minutes in the air fryer until the outside is gold-brown and squeaky-crisp. Pile onto a green salad with a drizzle of honey and a squeeze of lemon. Serves two.
sourceNotes: Halloumi's high melting point (made from a mix of cow, sheep, and goat milk with rennet) makes it ideal for dry-heat methods. The air-fryer timing here matches independent recipe-testing consensus across major UK food publications.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cooked halloumi turns rubbery from the freezer; eat the day it's cooked.
recipe.batchNotes: Cook in a single layer; doubling means two waves through the same drawer.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Slice the halloumi up to an hour ahead. The cooking has to happen at the last moment.

content.text: A 225 g block of halloumi cut into eight thick fingers, brushed with olive oil, slid into a pre-heated air-fryer drawer at 200°C, and cooked for seven minutes with a turn at four. Out gold-brown, squeaky on the bite, salty without being aggressive. Eaten on top of a green leaf salad with a drizzle of runny honey and a squeeze of lemon, or stuffed into a warm flatbread with rocket and tomato.

Halloumi behaves like no other cheese. The high melting point comes from the cooked-then-brined production; it browns dry without melting into a pool. The thicker the slice, the better the contrast between the gold crust and the soft squeaky inside. Don't slice it thin.

items.prepNote: one block, sliced into 8 fingers about 1 cm thick

content.text: Set the air fryer to 200°C and pre-heat for three minutes.

Cut {{halloumi}} of halloumi into eight 1 cm-thick fingers. Pat both sides dry with kitchen paper. Brush all over with {{olive-oil}} of olive oil and a twist of pepper. Halloumi is salty enough that no extra salt is needed.
Cook seven minutes with a turn

Lay the fingers in the air-fryer drawer in a single layer with space between them. Cook for 4 minutes. Open, turn each finger, and cook for a further 3 minutes until both sides are deep gold and the edges are crisp.
Pile the halloumi onto plates immediately. Drizzle with {{honey}} of honey if using; squeeze the {{lemon}} of lemon over the top. Eat hot — halloumi turns rubbery within five minutes off the heat.

items.cause: Sliced too thick, or air fryer not pre-heated.
items.fix: Pre-heat 3 minutes. Slice no thicker than 1 cm.
Turn at 4 minutes. The release happens at the colour change.
7 minutes is the maximum for 1 cm fingers. Set a timer.

items.symptom: Salty to the point of inedible
items.fix: Soak the cut fingers in cold water for 10 minutes; pat dry; cook.

content.text: Halloumi is Cyprus's gift to the air-fryer kitchen. Eat it as a starter, in a wrap, stuffed into a pitta with rocket and tomato, on top of a watermelon salad in July, or with roasted vegetables and a flatbread for a fast vegetarian dinner.
